supplementEntry.name: Shilajit
supplementEntry.evidenceTierRationale: Eight RCTs/clinical trials and one systematic review in humans, but most studies have small sample sizes (28-63), several come from the same research groups, and many use the same branded extract (PrimaVie). No large-scale independent replication for most endpoints.

nonCognitive: Collagen synthesis and connective tissue support
Spermatogenic activity and male fertility support
Skin microperfusion and extracellular matrix improvement

mechanismsOfAction: Fulvic acid-mediated mitochondrial electron transport enhancement
Antioxidant activity via MDA reduction and GSH increase
Extracellular matrix gene upregulation including collagen, elastin, fibronectin, and decorin
RANKL/OPG ratio modulation for bone metabolism
Hypothalamic-pituitary-gonadal axis modulation for testosterone support
Connective tissue adaptation via hydroxyproline modulation

commonSideEffects: No significant adverse effects reported across all trials
drugInteractions: May potentiate effects of antihypertensive medications
Theoretical interaction with anticoagulants due to platelet aggregation effects
Caution with hormone-sensitive conditions due to testosterone-increasing effects

effectSizes.collagenSynthesis: Large effect (d=1.2 at 500 mg/d, d=1.3 at 1000 mg/d) for pro-c1alpha1 biomarker (Neltner 2024, n=35)
effectSizes.fatigueStrengthRetention: 8.9% decline vs 16.0% placebo (p=0.044) in upper-strength subgroup (Keller 2019, n=63)
effectSizes.boneUnionTime: 129 vs 153 days, approximately 16% faster healing (p<0.049) (Sadeghi 2020, n=160)
effectSizes.testosterone: Significant increase (p<0.05) vs placebo over 90 days (Pandit 2016); +23.5% (p<0.001) in oligospermic men (Biswas 2010)
effectSizes.boneMineralDensity: Dose-dependent preservation vs placebo (p<0.001) over 48 weeks (Pingali 2022, n=60)
effectSizes.oxidativeStress: Significant MDA reduction (p<0.001) with 250 and 500 mg doses (Pingali 2022)

keyCitations.title: Effects of 8 Weeks of Shilajit Supplementation on Serum Pro-c1alpha1, a Biomarker of Type 1 Collagen Synthesis: A Randomized Control Trial

Shilajit extract reduces oxidative stress, inflammation, and bone loss to dose-dependently preserve bone mineral density in postmenopausal women with osteopenia: A randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial
Efficacy of Momiai in Tibia Fracture Repair: A Randomized Double-Blinded Placebo-Controlled Clinical Trial
keyCitations.journal: Journal of Alternative and Complementary Medicine

keyCitations.title: The effects of Shilajit supplementation on fatigue-induced decreases in muscular strength and serum hydroxyproline levels
keyCitations.journal: Journal of the International Society of Sports Nutrition

keyCitations.title: Skin Transcriptome of Middle-Aged Women Supplemented With Natural Herbo-mineral Shilajit Shows Induction of Microvascular and Extracellular Matrix Mechanisms
keyCitations.journal: Journal of the American College of Nutrition

keyCitations.title: Clinical evaluation of purified Shilajit on testosterone levels in healthy volunteers
Clinical evaluation of spermatogenic activity of processed Shilajit in oligospermia

mechanisms.mechanism: Fulvic acid-mediated mitochondrial electron transport enhancement

evidence.title: The Human Skeletal Muscle Transcriptome in Response to Oral Shilajit Supplementation
evidence.findings: 8 weeks of 250 mg bid shilajit upregulated 17 ECM-related gene probe sets in skeletal muscle including tenascin XB, decorin, myoferlin, collagen, elastin, fibrillin 1, and fibronectin 1. Well tolerated with no changes in blood glucose, lipid profile, creatine kinase, or serum myoglobin.
evidence.methodology: Transcriptomic analysis of skeletal muscle biopsies from overweight/class I obese adults

mechanisms.mechanism: RANKL/OPG ratio modulation for bone metabolism

evidence.title: Shilajit extract reduces oxidative stress, inflammation, and bone loss to dose-dependently preserve bone mineral density in postmenopausal women with osteopenia: A randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial
evidence.findings: Shilajit (250 mg or 500 mg) dose-dependently attenuated bone mineral density loss at lumbar spine and femoral neck vs placebo (p<0.001). Decreased CTX-1, BALP, RANKL; increased OPG. Significantly decreased MDA and hsCRP; increased GSH (all p<0.001).
evidence.methodology: Double-blind RCT in postmenopausal women aged 45-65 with osteopenia over 48 weeks

mechanisms.mechanism: Hypothalamic-pituitary-gonadal axis modulation for testosterone support

evidence.title: Clinical evaluation of purified Shilajit on testosterone levels in healthy volunteers
evidence.findings: 250 mg bid shilajit significantly (p<0.05) increased total testosterone, free testosterone, and DHEAS compared to placebo in healthy men aged 45-55. Gonadotropic hormones (LH, FSH) were maintained.
evidence.methodology: Double-blind RCT in healthy male volunteers aged 45-55

mechanisms.mechanism: Antioxidant activity via MDA reduction and GSH increase

evidence.title: Shilajit extract reduces oxidative stress, inflammation, and bone loss to dose-dependently preserve bone mineral density in postmenopausal women with osteopenia: A randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial
evidence.findings: Significantly decreased MDA and hsCRP; increased GSH (all p<0.001) with both 250 mg and 500 mg shilajit doses over 48 weeks.

evidence.title: Clinical evaluation of spermatogenic activity of processed Shilajit in oligospermia
evidence.findings: Semen MDA decreased by 18.7% with 200 mg/d processed shilajit over 90 days, alongside significant increases in sperm count (+61.4%) and testosterone (+23.5%).

benefits.replicationStatus: Single RCT with large effect sizes

evidence.title: Effects of 8 Weeks of Shilajit Supplementation on Serum Pro-c1alpha1, a Biomarker of Type 1 Collagen Synthesis: A Randomized Control Trial
evidence.findings: Both 500 mg/d and 1000 mg/d shilajit significantly increased serum pro-c1alpha1 (collagen synthesis biomarker) vs placebo. Low dose d=1.2 (p=0.008); high dose d=1.3 (p=0.007). 75% of high-dose subjects exceeded MCID vs 30% placebo (p=0.03).
evidence.methodology: Double-blind RCT in recreationally trained men

benefits.specificClaim: Dose-dependently preserves bone mineral density in postmenopausal women with osteopenia
benefits.replicationStatus: Supported by both bone density trial and fracture healing trial

evidence.title: Shilajit extract reduces oxidative stress, inflammation, and bone loss to dose-dependently preserve bone mineral density in postmenopausal women with osteopenia: A randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial
evidence.findings: Dose-dependent attenuation of bone mineral density loss at lumbar spine and femoral neck vs placebo (p<0.001). Decreased CTX-1, BALP, RANKL; increased OPG.
evidence.methodology: Double-blind RCT in postmenopausal women aged 45-65

evidence.title: Efficacy of Momiai in Tibia Fracture Repair: A Randomized Double-Blinded Placebo-Controlled Clinical Trial
evidence.journal: Journal of Alternative and Complementary Medicine
evidence.findings: Mean bone union time was 129 days (shilajit group) vs 153 days (placebo), approximately 16% faster healing (p<0.049). Dose: 1000 mg/d for 28 days. No significant difference in adverse effects.
evidence.methodology: Double-blind RCT in patients with tibia fractures

benefits.specificClaim: Increases total testosterone, free testosterone, and DHEAS in men
benefits.replicationStatus: Observed in both healthy men and oligospermic men, supported by systematic review

evidence.title: Clinical evaluation of purified Shilajit on testosterone levels in healthy volunteers
evidence.findings: Significant increase (p<0.05) in total testosterone, free testosterone, and DHEAS vs placebo in healthy men aged 45-55. Gonadotropic hormones (LH, FSH) maintained.
evidence.methodology: Double-blind RCT in healthy male volunteers

evidence.title: Clinical evaluation of spermatogenic activity of processed Shilajit in oligospermia
evidence.findings: Serum testosterone increased by 23.5% (p<0.001) with 200 mg/d processed shilajit. Total sperm count +61.4%, motility +12.4-17.4%.

evidence.title: Do 'testosterone boosters' really increase serum total testosterone? A systematic review
evidence.findings: Purified shilajit extract (PrimaVie) classified as 'possibly effective' for men with late-onset hypogonadism for increasing total testosterone. Most other testosterone boosters failed to increase testosterone.
evidence.methodology: Systematic review of 52 studies evaluating 27 testosterone boosters

benefits.specificClaim: Reduces fatigue-induced muscular strength decline and supports connective tissue adaptation

evidence.title: The effects of Shilajit supplementation on fatigue-induced decreases in muscular strength and serum hydroxyproline levels
evidence.journal: Journal of the International Society of Sports Nutrition
evidence.findings: High dose (500 mg/d): In upper 50th percentile strength group, post-fatigue MVIC decline was 8.9% vs 16.0% placebo (p=0.044). Baseline serum hydroxyproline significantly lower in high-dose group (1.5 vs 2.4 mcg/mL, p=0.024).
evidence.methodology: Double-blind RCT in recreationally active men

benefits.specificClaim: Improves sperm count, motility, and morphology in oligospermic men

evidence.title: Clinical evaluation of spermatogenic activity of processed Shilajit in oligospermia
evidence.findings: Total sperm count +61.4%, motility +12.4-17.4%, normal sperm count +18.9% with 200 mg/d processed shilajit. Liver and renal profiles unaffected.
evidence.methodology: Clinical trial in 35 enrolled (28 completed) oligospermic men

benefits.specificClaim: Induces microvascular and extracellular matrix gene expression in skin

evidence.title: Skin Transcriptome of Middle-Aged Women Supplemented With Natural Herbo-mineral Shilajit Shows Induction of Microvascular and Extracellular Matrix Mechanisms
evidence.journal: Journal of the American College of Nutrition
evidence.findings: 14 weeks of shilajit (125 or 250 mg bid) induced genes relevant to endothelial cell migration, blood vessel growth, and ECM in skin. Higher dose improved skin microperfusion. No adverse effects.
evidence.methodology: RCT with skin transcriptomic analysis in middle-aged women

safety.safetyAspect: General tolerability across multiple clinical trials
safety.claim: Good safety profile with no significant adverse events across all published human trials

evidence.title: Shilajit extract reduces oxidative stress, inflammation, and bone loss to dose-dependently preserve bone mineral density in postmenopausal women with osteopenia: A randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial
evidence.findings: No significant adverse events over 48 weeks at either 250 mg or 500 mg doses. Longest duration human safety data available.

evidence.title: Efficacy of Momiai in Tibia Fracture Repair: A Randomized Double-Blinded Placebo-Controlled Clinical Trial
evidence.journal: Journal of Alternative and Complementary Medicine
evidence.findings: No significant difference in adverse effects between shilajit (1000 mg/d) and placebo groups in the largest shilajit RCT to date.

evidence.title: The Human Skeletal Muscle Transcriptome in Response to Oral Shilajit Supplementation
evidence.findings: Well tolerated with no changes in blood glucose, lipid profile, creatine kinase, or serum myoglobin at 250 mg bid.
evidence.methodology: Clinical trial with comprehensive metabolic safety monitoring

dosage.dosageRange: 200-1000 mg/day of standardized purified extract, most commonly 500 mg/day
dosage.claim: Dosage range established across multiple clinical trials with dose-dependent effects observed

evidence.title: Effects of 8 Weeks of Shilajit Supplementation on Serum Pro-c1alpha1, a Biomarker of Type 1 Collagen Synthesis: A Randomized Control Trial
evidence.findings: Dose-response observed: 500 mg/d (d=1.2) and 1000 mg/d (d=1.3) both effective for collagen synthesis biomarker. 75% of high-dose subjects exceeded MCID.
evidence.methodology: Double-blind RCT comparing 500 mg/d, 1000 mg/d, and placebo

evidence.title: Shilajit extract reduces oxidative stress, inflammation, and bone loss to dose-dependently preserve bone mineral density in postmenopausal women with osteopenia: A randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial
evidence.findings: Dose-dependent effects observed: 500 mg outperformed 250 mg for bone mineral density preservation over 48 weeks.
evidence.methodology: Double-blind RCT comparing 250 mg, 500 mg, and placebo
